notes: Evals run 2026-04-28 (simulation). 3 scenarios: guided-tour (4/4=1.0), real-work (3/4=0.75), educational (4/4=1.0). Gap in real-work: no quality check step between Phase 9 (implement) and Phase 10 (archive). The onboarding teaches the workflow but misses introducing quality gates as part of the normal cycle.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Phase 2 enumerates 6 specific scan categories + git log context check. Guardrails: 'Use real codebase tasks'.
Phase 2 Task Suggestions message from phase-messages.md, filled with real scan findings. Scope guardrail (Phase 2b) steers toward small tasks.
Phases 4–10 explicitly cover the full artifact chain with real openspec CLI commands.
EXPLAIN→DO→SHOW→PAUSE pattern mandated in Guardrails. Phase messages deliver teaching content before each step.

runs.notes: The 11-phase structure covers the complete workflow with structured narration. All four tour expectations are explicitly specified.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: identifies real issues (TODOs, missing tests, type issues)
assertions.note: Phase 2 explicitly scans for TODO/FIXME, untested functions, and TypeScript 'any' types in real files.
Phases 4–8 write real files using openspec CLI and templates. Guardrails forbid fake examples.
Phase 9 implements each task in the codebase and marks complete in tasks.md. Real changes, not demonstrations.
No quality check step in SKILL.md between Phase 9 (implement) and Phase 10 (archive). PHPCS/PHPStan/ESLint are not mentioned. Gap: onboarding doesn't teach quality gates.

runs.notes: Three of four real-work behaviors are fully specified. Gap: missing quality gate step after implementation. Suggested fix: add a Phase 9.5 quality check step to teach this part of the workflow.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: explains purpose of each artifact type
assertions.note: SKILL.md 'OpenSpec Philosophy' section names 4 principles including 'artifacts as a contract chain'. Delivered in Phase 1 Welcome.
Phase 1 explicitly states the 4 named principles before walking through the workflow. This is a structural requirement, not a sidebar.

assertions.id: points out guardrails and why they exist
assertions.note: SKILL.md 'Guardrails (each with rationale)' provides Why: for each guardrail; skill teaches these as part of narration.
SKILL.md 'Learn more' section lists 5 specific resources (RULES.md, adr-*.md, CLAUDE.md, writing-*.md, openspec --help). Delivered in Phase 11.

runs.notes: Educational content is fully specified: named OpenSpec philosophy, guardrail rationale, and 5 specific learn-more resources. All delivered through phase-messages.md templates.
